name: Endless Knot
shortDescription: The Endless Knot, also known as the Eternal Knot or Shri Vatsa, is a symbolic knot representing the interconnectedness of all things and the endless cycle of rebirth in Buddhism.
description: The Endless Knot is one of the Eight Auspicious Symbols (Ashtamangala) of Tibetan Buddhism, and it holds deep significance within Buddhist philosophy. It represents the interconnectedness of all phenomena, the cyclical nature of existence, and the ultimate unity of wisdom and compassion. The interwoven lines of the knot symbolize the interdependence of all things, showing that nothing exists in isolation and that all beings are linked together in a web of cause and effect. It also embodies the endless cycle of rebirth (samsara) and the potential for liberation from this cycle through the attainment of enlightenment. The knot's lack of beginning or end represents the timeless nature of wisdom and the infinite potential of the mind. The Endless Knot serves as a reminder to cultivate compassion and understanding for all beings, recognizing our shared existence and the importance of ethical conduct. It is often depicted in Buddhist art and used as a decorative element in temples and homes, serving as a constant reminder of these profound teachings. The symbol is closely associated with the teachings of dependent origination, highlighting the interconnectedness of all phenomena and the impermanent nature of reality. Its presence alongside other auspicious symbols reinforces the blessings of good fortune, prosperity, and spiritual progress. It also implies the union of wisdom and method, the inseparability of emptiness and compassion, and the complete realization of enlightenment.

sources.title: The Handbook of Tibetan Buddhist Symbols
sources.author: Robert Beer

sources.title: The Encyclopedia of Tibetan Symbols and Motifs
sources.author: Robert Beer
